blocks.content: Webhooks let your application react to escrow state changes in real time. Vesicash sends an HTTP `POST` request to your configured endpoint whenever a relevant event occurs — such as funds being held, released, or a dispute being raised.

rows.name: escrow.created
rows.description: A new escrow transaction has been created and funds are being held.

rows.name: escrow.funded
rows.description: The buyer has funded the escrow successfully.

rows.name: escrow.released
rows.description: Funds have been released to the seller.

rows.name: escrow.refunded
rows.description: Funds have been returned to the buyer.

rows.name: escrow.dispute.opened
rows.description: A dispute has been raised on the transaction.

rows.name: escrow.expired
rows.description: The escrow window expired before conditions were met.

blocks.content: Every webhook includes an `x-vesicash-signature` header. Verify it against the raw request body using your webhook secret to ensure the payload came from Vesicash.
Always verify the webhook signature before processing. Reject any request that fails verification.
